ksa_text: Ability to apply consistent governance standards across heterogeneous AI systems — from simple rule-based automation to large language models — without treating all systems identically.

dcwf.top_element_label: Ability to apply programming language structures (e.g., source code review) and logic.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Ability to apply programming language structures (e.g., source code review) and logic.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AI-A-003 (AI and Machine Learning/Ability) scores 0.439 composite (highest: EU AI Act at 0.439) and is edge_case for the Statistician role. Core role signal: 0 pattern match(es). Seniority is appropriate for this role.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency identified. Include consistent with edge_case scope, EU AI Act signal (0.439), and AI and Machine Learning handling. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does this AI KSA reflect Statistician requirements?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Statistical ML (Bayesian methods, regression, predictive modeling) in 40-60% of Statistician postings. Deep learning engineering is not a core Statistician competency.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Statistical ML (Bayesian methods, regression, predictive modeling) in 40-60% of Statistician postings. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Statistical ML (Bayesian methods, regression, predictive modeling) in 40-60% of Statistician postings. Final decision: exclude.
